body:
Hey Ang, just a heads up — it's Monday which means the dreaded weekly status doc is due by EOD. I know you had that big client call this morning so I didn't want to text and interrupt, but figured an email was safe.

Also didn't you say last week you were going to finally templatize that thing so it stops taking you an hour? Did that ever happen lol

— Suki
snippet: Hey Ang, just a heads up — it's Monday which means the dreaded weekly status doc...
subject: weekly status doc reminder

body:
Ugh THANK YOU, I completely forgot. I've been in back-to-back calls since 9 and it totally slipped my mind. Submitting it now before I lose another window.

And no, the template did not happen. It's still the same chaotic Google Doc from March. I keep telling myself I'll fix it on a slow Friday and then Friday comes and I'm somehow busier than ever.

Will be home by 7, can we do takeout tonight? I'm too tired to even think about cooking — Ang
snippet: Ugh THANK YOU, I completely forgot. I've been in back-to-back calls since 9 and ...
subject: Re: weekly status doc reminder

Also I might be a little late getting home tonight, I have a call that's probably going to run long. Don't wait up lol.

Suki

subject: lunch this week??

body:

And no worries about tonight, I was just going to make that leftover pasta anyway. Pick up some wine on your way back if you have time?

Angela
snippet: Yes omg I've been wanting to go there forever!! Thursday works really well for m...

body:
Thursday at 12:30 is perfect!! I'll make a reservation just in case it gets crowded. So excited, we haven't done a proper lunch date in forever.

And YES to wine, I'll grab a bottle of that rosé you liked last time. See you tonight!

Suki
snippet: Thursday at 12:30 is perfect!! I'll make a reservation just in case it gets crow...
subject: Re: lunch this week??

body:
Hey Ang! So I've been working on redesigning my freelance portfolio site for like three weeks now and I finally have something I'm not completely embarrassed by. I know you do this stuff all day at work so I figured you'd be the best person to give me honest feedback.

I put together a Figma link — can you take a look whenever you get a chance? No rush, but I'm hoping to go live before the end of the month. Be brutal, I can take it.
snippet: Hey Ang! So I've been working on redesigning my freelance portfolio site for lik...
subject: Can you look at my portfolio redesign?

body:
Omg yes, send the link! I've been waiting for you to finally show me this — you've been so secretive about it lol.

I have a pretty light afternoon tomorrow so I can do a proper review then. Fair warning, I'm going to leave a lot of comments because that's just how my brain works, but it's all love. Also remind me what vibe you were going for — are you still targeting brand clients or did you shift back to UX work?
snippet: Omg yes, send the link! I've been waiting for you to finally show me this — you'...
subject: Re: Can you look at my portfolio redesign?

body:
Here's the link: figma.com/file/suki-portfolio-v3 (view access should work, let me know if it doesn't).

And yes, I shifted back to UX — brand work was paying okay but I was miserable. The whole thing is built around three case studies plus a short about page. I kept the color palette pretty minimal because I didn't want it to distract from the work itself. That said I'm second-guessing the hero section like every single day so please tell me if it's a mess.

Comment away, seriously. I'd rather know now than after I've bought a domain and told everyone.
snippet: Here's the link: figma.com/file/suki-portfolio-v3 (view access should work, let ...
subject: Re: Can you look at my portfolio redesign?

body:
Okay I went through everything and left comments in Figma — there are maybe 14 but most of them are small. Overall honestly? It's really good. The case study layout is clean and the hierarchy reads well. The hero is NOT a mess, you just need to tighten the headline copy and maybe bump the CTA button contrast a little.

The one thing I'd push back on is the about page photo — the lighting is a little dark and on mobile it feels cramped. Use the one from Mia's birthday, the one by the window. That's it, that's my note.

You're in good shape. Stop spiraling and go live. — Ang
snippet: Okay I went through everything and left comments in Figma — there are maybe 14 b...
subject: Re: Can you look at my portfolio redesign?

body:
Hey! Just wanted to check in because I need to book the Airbnb by tomorrow or we'll lose it. Are you still good for the Asheville trip the weekend of November 11th? The place I found sleeps 4 so I was also thinking of inviting Priya and Dev if you're cool with that.

Let me know ASAP so I can lock it in. It's $180/night so roughly $90 each for two nights if it's just us, less if the others come.
snippet: Hey! Just wanted to check in because I need to book the Airbnb by tomorrow or we...
subject: Weekend trip - are we still on?

body:
Yes absolutely still on, sorry I've been MIA this week — work has been insane. Book it!! $90/night each is totally reasonable for Asheville.

I'm fine with Priya and Dev coming, actually that might be more fun. Do you know if they're free? Also are we driving up Friday night or Saturday morning? I have a thing Friday until like 7 but I could leave by 8 if we want to make it a longer weekend.
snippet: Yes absolutely still on, sorry I've been MIA this week — work has been insane. B...
subject: Re: Weekend trip - are we still on?

body:
Booked it!! Sending you the confirmation. Priya is in, still waiting on Dev but he'll probably say yes.

I think Friday night makes more sense — even if we get there late it's better than losing Saturday morning. If you're done at 7 we could leave around 8:15? I can drive the first leg and you can take over after we stop for gas. It's only about 4 hours so we'd get there around midnight which is totally fine.

Also the Airbnb has a hot tub which I somehow missed when I booked it. We are so lucky.
snippet: Booked it!! Sending you the confirmation. Priya is in, still waiting on Dev but ...
subject: Re: Weekend trip - are we still on?

body:
A HOT TUB?? Okay this trip just went from fun to legendary. I'm very serious about spending at least 3 hours in it.

Friday at 8:15 works perfectly. I'll pack the night before so I'm not scrambling. Should we do a grocery run before we leave or just stop somewhere on the way? I'm thinking we grab snacks and breakfast stuff at minimum — eating out every single meal gets expensive fast. I can pick up wine and stuff if you handle the food basics?

Also can you send me the address so I can look up things to do nearby?
snippet: A HOT TUB?? Okay this trip just went from fun to legendary. I'm very serious abo...
subject: Re: Weekend trip - are we still on?

body:
Yes let's split it exactly like that — you bring drinks, I'll grab eggs, bread, fruit, and snacks. We can do one nice dinner out Saturday night and keep everything else casual.

Address is in the confirmation I forwarded but I'll text it to you too. There's a really good hiking trail like 10 minutes away and apparently the downtown area has a ton of breweries and a good farmers market on Saturday morning. Dev just confirmed he's coming btw so it'll be all four of us which honestly makes the hot tub situation even better.

See you Friday, I cannot wait to get out of the city!
snippet: Yes let's split it exactly like that — you bring drinks, I'll grab eggs, bread, ...
subject: Re: Weekend trip - are we still on?

Also, are we still splitting it 50/50? I can Venmo you my half this week if so. Let me know!

Suki

Angela

subject: Re: Netflix renewal reminder

body:
Hey, random ask but you're the only person I trust with this — my team is pushing me to sign off on the auth cutover this Friday and I'm not sure I'm comfortable with it. They want to migrate all users from the old OAuth flow to the new one in a single weekend window, no gradual rollout, just flip the switch Saturday night.

Does that sound insane to you? Like I know you've dealt with this stuff at finthesiss. Am I being too cautious or is my gut right that we should be pushing back?
snippet: Hey, random ask but you're the only person I trust with this — my team is pushin...
subject: auth cutover plan

body:
Your gut is right, do NOT let them do a big bang cutover on a Friday night with no rollout strategy. That is genuinely one of the scariest things you can do with auth. If anything goes sideways — token incompatibility, session invalidation issues, whatever — you're locked out of prod on a weekend with half the team unavailable.

At minimum you want a canary phase, even if it's just 5% of users for 24 hours so you can catch edge cases before they're everyone's problem. Also make sure you have a rollback plan that's actually been tested, not just theoretically possible.

Send me their migration doc if you have it, I'll take a look tonight. We can talk through it over dinner if you want.
snippet: Your gut is right, do NOT let them do a big bang cutover on a Friday night with ...
subject: Re: auth cutover plan

body:
Okay yes thank you, I felt like I was being annoying for asking questions but this validates everything. I just sent you the doc — warning it is not great, the rollback section is literally one bullet point that says 'revert deployment.'

I'm going to push for a two-week delay and a proper phased rollout. If they fight me on it I'm going to forward them your message lol. Dinner sounds perfect, I'll grab the good wine. You're the best.
snippet: Okay yes thank you, I felt like I was being annoying for asking questions but th...
subject: Re: auth cutover plan
